title: Dashboard shows zero stats on load — missing skeleton/loading states

items.title: Create BootstrapGate wrapper component
items.description: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/BootstrapGate.tsx — a wrapper component that checks selectIsBootstrapped from the Zustand store and renders either children (when bootstrapped) or a fallback skeleton. Simple pattern: `const ready = useDashboardStore(selectIsBootstrapped); return ready ? children : fallback;`
acceptance: Component accepts children and fallback ReactNode props
Renders fallback when bootstrapComplete is false, children when true

items.title: Create shimmer skeleton components for each view
items.description: Create skeleton placeholder components with animate-pulse shimmer for: (1) KanbanSkeleton — 4-5 column layout with card-shaped placeholders, (2) AgentListSkeleton — list of row-shaped placeholders, (3) GodViewSkeleton — grid of card placeholders, (4) HeaderSkeleton — placeholder for sidebar count badges. Use Tailwind animate-pulse bg-surface-2 rounded classes. Match the approximate layout shape of each real component.
acceptance: Each skeleton visually approximates the layout of its corresponding real component
All use animate-pulse for shimmer effect
Use existing Tailwind theme tokens (bg-surface-2, rounded, etc.)
No external dependencies — pure Tailwind CSS

items.title: Wire BootstrapGate into App.tsx and Header
items.description: In App.tsx, wrap store-consuming tab views (kanban, agents, god-view) with <BootstrapGate fallback={<XxxSkeleton />}>. Also gate the Header's agent/issue count badges so they don't flash '0' before bootstrap. The MetricsSummaryRow on the kanban tab should also be inside the gate since it sits above the KanbanBoard.
acceptance: Kanban tab shows KanbanSkeleton until bootstrapComplete
Agents tab shows AgentListSkeleton until bootstrapComplete
Header count badges don't show 0 during bootstrap
React Query views (MissionControl, HandoffsPage, etc.) are NOT wrapped — they have their own loading states

items.title: Verify build and visual test
items.description: Run npm run build to ensure no type errors or build failures. Visually verify in the browser that: (1) skeletons appear on fresh page load, (2) skeletons transition smoothly to real content when snapshot arrives, (3) no flash of zeros/empty state, (4) React Query views still work independently with their own loading states.
acceptance: npm run build succeeds with no errors
No flash of zero/empty state on page load
Skeleton → real content transition is smooth
Existing React Query loading states unchanged
